items.title: PWHL expands into Hamilton
items.summary_auto: Representatives from the PWHL, Oak View Group, the City of Hamilton and more discuss the PWHL’S expansion to Hamilton. The league now has 11 teams with Hamilton joining Toronto, Ottawa, Montreal and Vancouver as the newest Canadian entry.

items.title: Here's why you don’t need to worry about a 'super' El Niño — yet
items.summary_auto: There’s been a lot of talk about the possibility of a "super" El Niño developing this year, but scientists say not to expect impacts in Canada this summer. That doesn’t mean we get off scott-free.

items.title: Edmonton Oilers fire head coach Kris Knoblauch: reports
items.summary_auto: The Edmonton Oilers have fired head coach Kris Knoblauch,  according to multiple reports on Thursday.

items.title: Carney government to announce details of plan to double electricity grid
Deer Lake, N.L., bracing for flooding, as mayor says water levels highest in 20 years
items.summary_auto: “We are not in a good position,” said Mayor Dean Ball, about the situation that will see Deer Lake Power open its spill gates over the upcoming long weekend into the Grand Lake Reservoir, which is already seeing high water levels due to melting snow and rain.

items.title: Honda indefinitely suspends $15B EV plant in Ontario
items.summary_auto: The project, first announced in 2024, was paused last year with plans to review where the EV market was in two years. The indefinite pause comes as Honda posted its first-ever full-year loss.

items.title: Zelenskyy's former top aide arrested as Ukraine graft probe widens
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's former right-hand man was taken into custody on Thursday on money-laundering charges, part of a fight against graft that is fueling wartime political tensions.

items.title: Biocontrol facility takes aim at invasive insect threatening forest
items.summary_auto: The hemlock woolly adelgid — named because it is covered in white woolly material — can cause foliage loss and even kill eastern hemlock trees. A team at Nova Scotia's Acadia University are rearing specialized predatory insects they're hoping can control these pests. The CBC's Gareth Hampshire suited up to get into the facility.

items.title: Cricket Canada's funding frozen by governing body following fifth estate investigation
Here’s where home invasions are happening in Toronto and what’s being done to prevent them
items.summary_auto: The rise in home invasions in Toronto over the last few years is largely due to a significant increase in home invasions involving houses in some of the city's wealthiest neighbourhoods, according to a CBC News analysis of Toronto police data. Residents of those neighbourhoods have been taking matters into their own hands.

items.title: Canada wants sovereign AI data centres. What does that actually mean?
items.summary_auto: Canada wants to build data centres that are not just physically located here, but controlled here — a distinction experts say could determine whether the country can reduce its dependence on U.S. tech giants and keep Canadian data subject to Canadian rules.

items.title: These refugees in Kenya were promised jobs in Canada. At the 11th hour, the employer cancelled the offer
items.summary_auto: A continuing care organization in Nova Scotia has rescinded job offers it had been extending to refugees for more than two years, leaving the displaced people without a path out of some of the largest refugee camps in the world. They were invited to come to Canada through a federal program, but the organization says as processing timelines and the number of people needing care increased, many of the roles were filled locally.

items.title: How fires are permanently displacing Montrealers and reshaping neighbourhoods
items.summary_auto: When fires break out in multi-unit buildings in Montreal, tenants vacate and are taken in by the Red Cross. But what happens next can often be a difficult process to navigate for renters. Housing advocates worry these fires are leading to permanent displacement of tenants — and reshaping neighbourhoods in the process.

items.title: As Canada plans to send election observers to Armenia, advocates warn of a democratic backslide
items.summary_auto: As the federal government prepares to send seven election observers to Armenia, an advocacy group featuring the former president of Human Rights Watch is warning Canada and its Western allies of democratic backsliding prior to the June vote.

items.title: Lawyers across Canada are mounting constitutional challenges to new asylum law
items.summary_auto: Immigration lawyers across Canada are co-ordinating a constitutional challenge against Ottawa's new asylum law, with three dozen cases already filed in Federal Court.

items.title: Travel to the U.S. is down even more dramatically than we thought, data shows
items.summary_auto: The boycott by Canadians of travel to the U.S., driven largely by an aversion to President Donald Trump, his industry-wrecking tariffs and 51st state taunts, has actually been more effective than previously thought, according to new data.

items.title: Sobeys, Loblaw under fire for maple washing, as Sobeys ditches maple leaf symbol in stores
items.summary_auto: More than a year after the Buy Canadian movement took root, grocery giants Loblaw and Sobeys are facing increased scrutiny over "maple washing" — the practice of promoting imported goods as homegrown.

items.title: Ontario starts crackdown on ticket resale above face value
items.summary_auto: Letters notifying resellers of inspection were sent out last week, and investigators are starting to inspect ticket resellers and big companies like StubHub this week, according to Minister of Public and Business Service Delivery and Procurement Stephen Crawford. But resale sites say they're still looking for guidance on how to implement the rules.

items.title: Toronto Tempo celebrate 1st-ever WNBA win in franchise history
items.summary_auto: The expansion Toronto Tempo earned their first win in the WNBA by defeating the Seattle Storm 86-73 on Wednesday night.

items.title: Why British PM Keir Starmer is fighting to keep his job
items.summary_auto: Less than two years after leading his Labour Party to a massive victory, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is hugely unpopular and fighting to keep his job. For The National, CBC’s Margaret Evans breaks down what’s behind his political problems.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | A rare guided tour of the CN Tower's off-limit areas
items.summary_auto: The CN Tower is giving rare access to areas of the engineering marvel that are normally closed to the public. CBC News photographer Evan Mitsui checked out the tower's lesser known parts during a media preview on Wednesday.

items.title: Marco Rubio's new Chinese name | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains how China's government gave U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio a new, slightly tweaked, name — one that doesn't ban him from entering China. (Photo credits: The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images)

items.title: Just over 4 British Columbians died of toxic drugs daily in March: coroner
items.summary_auto: The B.C. Coroners Service says 135 people died in March due to suspected unregulated drug poisoning, up from 115 deaths the month before.

items.title: Did Jeffrey Epstein really introduce Donald Trump and Melania?
items.summary_auto: Who introduced Donald Trump and his wife, Melania Trump? For years, Italian entrepreneur and close Trump associate Paolo Zampolli has taken credit, but the release of the Epstein files surfaced an FBI document that said it was financier and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. What’s the truth? CBC’s visual investigations team and Katie Nicholson dig into the files to reveal surprising new details.

items.title: Stage is set for the PWHL's 1st all-Canadian Walter Cup final
items.summary_auto: The best-of-five Walter Cup final series between the Montreal Victoire and Ottawa Charge will begin on Thursday, ensuring the Cup is won by a Canadian team for the first time in history.

items.title: Sheinbaum denies CIA involvement in assassination of cartel operative on Mexican soil
items.summary_auto: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said on Wednesday that media reports claiming the CIA was involved in a recent targeted car bombing outside Mexico City were "fictions the size of the universe."

items.title: Animal therapy for Gaza’s displaced children
items.summary_auto: After rescuing abandoned pets from the streets of Gaza, Rashid Anbar gave the animals a second chance at life and a new purpose: providing support and joy to displaced Palestinian children in the war-torn territory.

items.title: Utah woman handed life sentence for fentanyl-laced-cocktail murder of husband
items.summary_auto: A Utah mother who published a children's book about grief after the death of her husband will serve a life sentence without the possibility of parole for his murder, a judge ruled Wednesday.

items.title: FAA adding transponders to all its airport vehicles after Air Canada plane's deadly LaGuardia runway crash
items.summary_auto: The agency in charge of aviation safety in the U.S. says all of its airport vehicles will be equipped with technology to help air-traffic controllers track their movements, a move expedited by the deadly crash in March involving an Air Canada jet at New York's LaGuardia Airport.

items.title: Judge overturns Elections Alberta’s approval of separation referendum petition
items.summary_auto: An Alberta Court of King’s Bench justice has quashed Elections Alberta’s approval of a petition for a referendum on Alberta independence from separatist group Stay Free Alberta.

items.title: There are many questions about why a coyote swam to Alcatraz. One theory? It was looking for love
items.summary_auto: Camilla Fox has been fascinated by wild canines for most of her life, even helping to raise an orphaned wolf pup as a child. But the journey of the so-called “Alcatraz Coyote” is unlike anything she has seen before.

items.title: Islanders' Matthew Schaefer first unanimous NHL rookie of the year recipient since 1993
items.summary_auto: New York Islanders defenceman Matthew Schaefer has been awarded the Calder Memorial Trophy as the NHL's top rookie.

items.title: U.S. Senate votes to approve Trump's pick for Federal Reserve chair
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Senate has confirmed Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's nominee to lead the Federal Reserve, bringing new leadership to the world's most powerful central bank at a fraught moment for the global economy.

items.title: Retrial set for lawyer Alex Murdaugh after convictions overturned in deaths of wife, son
items.summary_auto: The murder convictions and life sentence of Alex Murdaugh were overturned Wednesday by the South Carolina Supreme Court because the court clerk at his trial suggested he was guilty, but the disgraced lawyer won't be leaving prison any time soon.

items.title: At least 4 killed, several missing as Russian barrage destroys several residential buildings in Ukraine
items.summary_auto: A massive Russian drone and missile bombardment of Ukraine that began before dawn Thursday demolished an apartment block in Kyiv, the capital city where four people were killed and 33 wounded, authorities said

items.title: Drug counsellor who delivered fatal ketamine doses to Matthew Perry gets 2 years in prison
items.summary_auto: A licensed drug addiction counsellor who delivered Friends star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him receivd his sentence on Wednesday.

items.title: Quebec proposes law allowing people to obtain intimate partner violence history for partners
items.summary_auto: Bill 4, tabled on Wednesday, would allow people who fear for their safety or that of their children to obtain information about the conjugal violence history of their current or ex-partner.

items.title: Truck driver in crash that killed former Olympic figure skater sentenced to prison time
items.summary_auto: The truck driver who pleaded guilty to dangerous driving charges in an Ontario crash that killed a former Canadian Olympic figure skater and injured multiple people was sentenced to two and a half years in prison on Wednesday.
